opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: This rash started with one big spot about a week ago that I just ignored, and now my whole torso is covered in these scaly red marks. It's spread so fast, I'm terrified it's something serious spreading through my body — like it's going to get into my organs.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It exploded across my chest and back in the last day or two and I panicked — I need to know it's not something dangerous or contagious.

symptom_narrative.onset: about a week ago it started with one big scaly patch on my side, then in the last 48 hours loads of smaller marks came up
symptom_narrative.duration: a week for the first patch, two days for the widespread part
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is mild to moderate, I can cope with it, but the look of it is really scaring me
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over my chest, back and tummy
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the itch is worse after a hot shower
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: hot showers make the itching flare up
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just the itch really — I feel otherwise okay in myself
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm so distracted and anxious about it I can't concentrate, but I'm still managing day to day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and feels very distressed being on my own with a rash I can't explain

objective_data_offered.value: on video: lots of small oval scaly pink-red marks scattered across my chest, back and tummy, and one bigger scaly patch about the size of a 50c coin on my side
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone camera in home lighting — colour and fine detail of the marks are hard to judge remotely

objective_data_offered.value: 37.0 C on my home thermometer this morning
